title: Letterhead Memorandum
source_text: 4. Letterhead Memorandum. The letterhead memorandum may be used within your activity and provides more formality than the printed form or plain-paper memorandum. When direct liaison with individuals outside of your activity is authorized, the letterhead memorandum may be used to correspond on routine matters that neither make a commitment nor take an official stand. A full signature line is not required because the "From:" line identifies the signer. See figure 10-4. For example: You have been appointed chairperson of a committee and need to correspond with other members of the committee outside your activity.
plain_language_rule: The letterhead memorandum may be used within your activity and provides more formality than the printed form or plain-paper memorandum. When direct liaison with individuals outside of your activity is authorized, the letterhead memorandum may be used to correspond on routine matters that neither make a commitment nor take an official stand. A full signature line is not required because the 'From:' line identifies the signer. Figure 10-4 shows a letterhead memorandum with letterhead at the top. Sender's symbols appear under the letterhead on the right: SSIC (e.g., 5216), Memo serial (e.g., Memo 00/83), and Date (e.g., 5 Jan 15). Heading is 'MEMORANDUM.' Figure 10-4 shows From, To, and Subj fields. Figure 10-4 confirms when used within an activity, the letterhead memorandum provides more formality than the printed memorandum form or plain-paper memorandum. Figure 10-4 confirms a letterhead memorandum may be sent outside your activity if: direct liaison is authorized, the matter is routine, and the memo neither makes a commitment nor takes an official stand. Figure 10-4 says generally follow the standard letter format, but type 'MEMORANDUM' as shown. Figure 10-4 shows the writer signs by name only.
renderer_impact: Render letterhead at top of page. Place sender's symbols under letterhead on right side: SSIC, Memo serial, and Date. Type heading as 'MEMORANDUM.' Support From, To, and Subj fields. Follow standard letter format layout. Render signature by name only (no organizational titles; 'From:' line identifies signer). Figure 10-4 is rule-bearing for layout and field placement.
notes: Source text and Figure 10-4 details resolved from manual section 10-2.4. This rule governs the letterhead memorandum format, which provides more formality than printed or plain-paper memorandums. Internal use within activity is standard. External use permitted only when: (1) direct liaison is authorized, (2) matter is routine, and (3) memo makes no commitment or official stand. Full signature line not required since 'From:' identifies signer. Sender's symbols (SSIC, Memo serial, Date) positioned under letterhead on right. MEMORANDUM heading required. Standard letter format followed with MEMORANDUM typed as heading. Figure 10-4 is rule-bearing.
